data.goal: Critical review of Pulse implementation - is this the best we can build?

data.description: Critical review of Pulse reveals it's a well-conceived first draft but NOT at the level it needs to be. Research is too shallow, no disqualification logic, delivery has fingerprinting bugs, no feedback loop wired up.
data.research_rating: 5/10 - homepage only, random competitors, no JS rendering, no company name extraction, no disqualification
data.generation_rating: 7/10 - strong prompts but limited by shallow research data
data.delivery_rating: 6/10 - good instincts but X-Mailer leak, timezone bug, no bounce handling, no unsubscribe
data.verdict: One good iteration away from being genuinely effective. Need to fix the foundations before shipping.

data.description: Email infrastructure: Resend API for sending, Gmail for inbox, Cloudflare for custom domain email. Primary goal: inbox placement, not spam/promotions.
data.reasoning: Resend has excellent deliverability, proper DKIM/SPF/DMARC out of the box with Cloudflare domains. Gmail inbox is where replies land. The entire system must produce emails indistinguishable from hand-written personal correspondence.
data.key_constraint: Must land in primary inbox, not spam or promotions tab

data.goal: Thorough audit of Pulse project - bugs, type errors, inconsistencies, schema issues, Resend migration

data.description: Completed thorough audit of Pulse project - found 11 distinct issues across stale docs, SMTP messaging, hardcoded values, schema alignment, and type risks

Pulse v1 sprint complete — all 15 fix items shipped. Research pipeline: multi-page scraping, deterministic competitors, disqualification gate, domain dedup, company name extraction. Synthesis: 5 exemplars, random selection for voice variety, critique feedback into retries, lazy model initialization. Delivery: Resend API (replaces nodemailer), plain text only, no fingerprints, timezone-aware business hours, blacklist/unsubscribe, bounce handling. Learning loop and reply tracking scaffolded. Final identity check passed.
data.identity_check: passed — system reflects Aslam's craft philosophy
data.remaining: homepage double-fetch performance issue, IMAP automation, Resend webhook integration

data.description: Second-pass quality improvements to Pulse email synthesis system — 6 deep changes that improve email quality, not just engineering correctness
data.changes: 1. Scraper: single-fetch-per-page, rawHtml preserved, OG metadata + Schema.org structured data extraction. 2. Analyzer: findStrengths() detects heritage, specialization, certifications, location, story — tracks whether leveraged or buried. 3. Generator prompt: includes hidden strengths section so LLM can write 'you have X but your site doesn't show it'. 4. Subject line guidance: voice.json now has rules, good/bad examples — 'a subject line is a window, not a billboard'. 5. Philosophy escape hatch: when NOT to send (already differentiated, different problem, already has a partner). 6. OG data passed to generator for richer context.
data.trigger: User challenged 'but is it the best it could be?' after initial sprint was complete

data.description: Pulse upgraded from single-email sender to full sales pipeline — follow-up sequences, conversation tracking, reply playbook, learning feedback loop, cost-of-inaction in prompt
data.what_changed: 1. Follow-up sequence system: 3-touch emails (Mirror → Evidence → Door Close). Database tracks sequence_position on drafts and sent_emails. Follow-up generator has separate prompts for each position. 2. Conversations table + tracker: Full pipeline from outreach → replied → discovery → proposal → won/lost/dead. Auto-marks dead conversations. 3. Reply playbook (brain/playbook.md): What to do for each reply type (interested, curious-but-guarded, defensive, asking-for-free-work). Includes discovery call structure, pricing tiers, conversion metrics. 4. Generator prompt: Now includes cost-of-inaction, credibility line (imaslam.com), lowest-friction close (yes/no question). 5. Learning feedback: Research pipeline now prioritizes industries with higher reply rates. 6. New CLI: npm run followup, npm run pipeline (status/actions/advance/note/reply). 7. Dashboard shows pipeline summary.
data.why: User asked 'what happens when someone replies?' — the system had no answer. Now it does: playbook for each reply type, pipeline stages mapping to 9-phase brand process, pricing tiers, and automated follow-up generation.

data.description: Follow-up sequence is 3 emails max, not infinite. Door Close (email 3) works by giving permission to say no, which paradoxically increases replies.
data.why: Principle 8 says 'Persistence Without Intelligence Is Spam.' Three emails with escalating specificity is the sweet spot. Email 1 = mirror (the diagnosis). Email 2 = evidence (deeper finding, 80-100 words, new value). Email 3 = door close (30-50 words, permission to say no). More than 3 burns the relationship. Fewer than 3 leaves money on the table — 80% of replies come from follow-ups.
data.alternatives_considered: Considered 5-email sequence but it violates the Respect The Inbox principle. Considered 2 emails but the door close is where most responses come from.

data.goal: Understand Narrative Engine's research, data gathering, and intelligence capabilities

data.description: Completed comprehensive exploration of Narrative Engine's research, data gathering, and intelligence architecture
Pulse must be autonomous — not a CLI tool. Build heartbeat loop that runs research, synthesis, follow-ups, and sends without manual commands. Add Google search for deeper research beyond scraping.
data.why: Aslam's pattern is building autonomous systems (Narrative Engine heartbeat). A CLI tool requiring 6 manual commands contradicts his identity. He needs systems that work while he builds ElimuAfrica. The Narrative Engine v2 deterministic consciousness is the blueprint: math for behavioral decisions, LLM only for creative work.
data.research_gap: Web scraping alone gives shallow observations. Need Google search results about the company (reviews, mentions, social media) to write emails that feel like genuine research, not automated scanning.

data.description: Migrate Pulse from @google/generative-ai (deprecated Dec 2025) to @google/genai. Use Gemini's built-in Google Search grounding for prospect research instead of adding Tavily.
data.why: Same API key, no new costs, no new dependencies. Search grounding lets Gemini find reviews, social media, news articles about the prospect during research. The older SDK is archived and read-only.
data.model_for_search: gemini-2.0-flash for search-grounded research calls, same model for generation and critique
data.risk: groundingMetadata can sometimes be empty with flash models, but the text response still contains grounded information

data.description: Built autonomous heartbeat loop for Pulse. Single process runs: Research → Synthesize → Auto-approve (score >= 85) → Send (Poisson delays, business hours) → Follow-ups → Learn → Sleep → Repeat. Only human gate: drafts below 85 need manual review via `npm run approve`. Deep research (Google Search grounding) fully integrated into pipeline, audit storage, generator prompt, and follow-up prompts.
data.files_modified: src/research/orchestrator.ts, src/synthesis/generator.ts, src/synthesis/followup.ts, src/cli/approve.ts, src/cli/dashboard.ts, package.json

data.description: Auto-approve threshold set at 85/100 critique score. Drafts at or above this score are sent autonomously. Below 85 requires manual review. This balances quality (the critique system already enforces standards) with autonomy (Aslam doesn't want to click things).
data.why: 85 is the sweet spot — the critique system already filters hard (specificity, voice, length, differentiation quality). If a draft passes at 85+, it's genuinely good. Below that, human eyes catch what the critic missed.
data.alternatives_considered: 90 (too strict, most drafts would need review), 75 (too loose, risky quality)

data.description: Switching Pulse's creative LLM from Gemini to Claude. Claude for all writing (email synthesis, critique, follow-ups) — its voice is more direct, more specific, more human, closer to Aslam's actual tone. Keeping Gemini ONLY for Google Search grounding (deep research) since that's a unique capability Claude doesn't have.
data.why: Emails are the product. Gemini writes like a helpful assistant. Claude writes like a person with opinions. Aslam's brand voice is direct, sharp, no filler — Claude matches that better.
data.alternatives_considered: All-Claude (lose Google Search grounding), All-Gemini (weaker creative writing), Gemini for everything with heavy prompt engineering (fighting the model instead of working with it)

data.description: Switched Pulse's creative LLM from Gemini to Claude. Claude Sonnet handles all writing (email synthesis, critique, follow-ups). Gemini stays only for Google Search grounding in deep research. The shared llm.ts abstraction meant zero changes to consuming files — generate() now routes to Claude, generateWithSearch() stays on Gemini.

Deep research integration completed. deepResearchResult now saved to audit DB, fed into generator prompt (reviews, hidden gems, social presence, market position), and fed into follow-up email generator. Generator prompt now instructs LLM to lead with hidden gems when available.

Built autonomous heartbeat loop. Single process: Research → Synthesize → Auto-approve (score >= 85) → Send (Poisson delays, business hours) → Follow-ups → Learn → Sleep 60min → Repeat. Flags: --dry-run, --once. Updated approve CLI with all/reject commands.

data.goal: Conduct thorough audit of Pulse codebase to identify real problems in email generation, research pipeline, and delivery systems

data.description: Audit completed: 5-7 critical structural problems identified in email generation, research, and critique systems affecting reply rates
data.summary: Problems span generator prompt specificity, critique rubber-stamp effect, research data richness, brain context depth, and email scoring thresholds

data.description: Deep audit revealed 7 structural problems in Pulse that would cap reply rates at 2-5% instead of 15-20%: (1) Generator prompt is vague — gives options instead of a decision tree for opening, (2) Critique is a rubber stamp — weighted scoring lets mediocre emails through, needs hard gates, (3) Research doesn't provide structured specificity ammunition, (4) Brain files are abstract philosophy not enforceable constraints, (5) Auto-approve threshold (85) is arbitrary with no feedback loop, (6) Deep research findings suggested not required in email opening, (7) Follow-up emails have no enforcement of "new material" requirement.
data.impact: reply rates capped at 2-5% vs potential 15-20%
data.root_cause: system is technically correct but emotionally flat — prompts allow too much LLM discretion instead of constraining output

data.description: Fixed all 7 structural quality problems in Pulse. (1) Generator prompt now uses a decision tree — IF hidden gems → MUST open with them, ELSE IF buried strengths → MUST open with those, ELSE sharpest gap. No discretion. (2) Critique now has hard gates (deterministic, no LLM): banned words, word count, must end with question, no sales closes, must reference verifiable audit data, subject line checks. Gates run BEFORE LLM scoring — fail any gate = instant reject. (3) Deep research findings forced into opening via the decision tree. (4) Critique criteria redesigned: 6 criteria focused on impact (opening_hook, diagnosis_depth, voice_authenticity, cost_clarity, close_quality, would_you_reply) with harsh scoring instructions. (5) Follow-up prompt now has prioritized option list (A→B→C→D→E) instead of vague "add new value." (6) Learning loop now tracks: 5-point score brackets (90+, 85-89, 80-84, 75-79, <75), sequence position performance, and auto-approved vs manual-approved reply rates.
data.approach: deterministic gates + prescriptive prompts — constrain LLM output instead of suggesting

data.description: LLM prompts that say 'consider' or 'if available, lead with' produce inconsistent output. The model treats suggestions as optional. Decision trees (IF X → MUST do Y, ELSE → MUST do Z) produce consistently strong output. Same principle applies to quality gates: deterministic checks (word in banned list? reject) are more reliable than asking an LLM to score how well an email avoids generic language.
data.context: Discovered during audit that generator prompt gave 3 conflicting opening strategies as suggestions. LLM picked randomly, producing high variance. Decision tree eliminated variance.

data.description: Pulse's learning system is a dead loop. Learnings are calculated (industry reply rates, score brackets, timing) and stored in DB, but NOTHING reads them back. The generator doesn't know which industries respond better. The critique doesn't know which opening styles get replies. The brain doesn't grow. The system sends email #100 with the same knowledge it had at email #1.
data.impact: System never improves. Every email is generated with zero learning from previous sends. This is the opposite of what Aslam builds.
data.what_should_happen: Learnings should feed back into: (1) generator prompt — what opening styles work, (2) critique criteria — weight what actually correlates with replies, (3) brain exemplars — successful emails become new exemplars, (4) industry-specific voice adjustments

data.description: Built Pulse's intelligence layer — the system now accumulates knowledge and gets smarter with every email sent. Three feedback loops: (1) Emails that get replies are auto-promoted to exemplars — the brain literally grows from what works. Proven exemplars get 60% selection priority over handwritten ones. (2) Industry intelligence injected into generator prompt — if tourism responds at 15% but restaurants at 2%, the system writes differently for each, with explicit coaching. (3) Critique calibration from real data — the system tracks which scoring criteria actually correlate with replies and tells the critique LLM to weight those more heavily.
data.key_insight: The brain grows. Email #100 is generated with knowledge from emails 1-99. The system learns what opening styles, industries, and score brackets actually produce replies.

data.description: Pulse lacks rich context accumulation. It has a pipeline (scrape → analyze → write) but no world model. No knowledge graph per prospect. No structured understanding of who a company is, what their story is, what the market narrative around them looks like. The Narrative Engine had memory tiers, knowledge graphs, behavioral state — Pulse has SQL tables of reply rates. This is the difference between an email tool and something we'd be proud of.
data.what_narrative_engine_had: knowledge graph, memory tiers (working/episodic/semantic/procedural), behavioral state, temporal awareness, narrative arc tracking
data.what_pulse_has: flat SQL tables, raw scraped text, pattern counting
data.gap: Pulse doesn't know companies — it processes them

data.goal: Build rich context system for Pulse — knowledge graph, memory tiers, world model per prospect. Make it something to be proud of.

data.description: Designing rich context system for Pulse — knowledge graph, memory tiers, world model per prospect. Adapting Narrative Engine's architecture for an outreach system context.
data.reference: narrative-engine knowledge graph, memory tiers, evidence store, world model
data.gap: Pulse has flat SQL + JSON blobs, no structured world knowledge
data.approach: Adapt NE patterns for outreach: prospect world model, industry knowledge graph, market memory tiers, competitive intelligence accumulation

data.description: Built Pulse world model with 4 layers: knowledge graph (nodes + edges + memory tiers), prospect memory (structured profiles), industry memory (sector intelligence), market memory (cross-company patterns). Adapted Narrative Engine's architecture for outreach context.
data.why: User challenged that Pulse processes companies but doesn't KNOW them. Referenced Narrative Engine's knowledge graph and memory tiers as the standard.
data.architecture: SQLite-based (no Postgres/ChromaDB dependency), 5 new tables (knowledge_nodes, knowledge_edges, prospect_profiles, industry_profiles, market_patterns, interaction_events), 3 memory tiers (working/consolidated/deep) with decay and promotion
data.alternatives_rejected: Considered porting Narrative Engine's Prisma/Postgres/ChromaDB stack directly, rejected because Pulse is SQLite-based and should stay lightweight
data.key_innovation: findSimilarNodes uses 3-word phrase matching for dedup/reinforcement without requiring vector embeddings

data.description: Pulse world model built and wired into pipeline. 5 new files in src/memory/, 6 new DB tables, integrated into research orchestrator, generator, and heartbeat. Clean TypeScript build.
data.tables_added: knowledge_nodes, knowledge_edges, prospect_profiles, industry_profiles, market_patterns, interaction_events

data.description: Pulse needs to be a branding expert, not an email machine. The knowledge gap isn't about prospects — it's about the CRAFT. Pulse should know branding, positioning, digital experience design the way a 10-year veteran knows it. Not from LLM synthesis but from accumulated, structured domain expertise. The target isn't reply rates — it's 1 in 3 emails turning into a paycheck, which requires surgical precision in WHO to email and HOW to diagnose.
data.insight: Narrative Engine was first attempt, Pulse must surpass it. Every aspect should be more inventive than anything we've done.
data.reframe: From email outreach tool to autonomous branding specialist

data.description: Domain expertise fully wired into both generator and critique pipelines. Generator now injects relevant expertise docs (scored by audit findings) into the synthesis prompt. Critique now has a 'domain_expertise' criterion (15% weight) that evaluates whether emails demonstrate real branding knowledge vs surface observations. The critique prompt also receives expertise context so the LLM scorer knows what genuine expertise looks like. TypeScript clean.

User correctly identified that expertise markdown docs are just fancy prompt engineering — Claude synthesizes from them, Pulse doesn't actually KNOW anything. The expertise needs to live in the knowledge graph as nodes that get reinforced by real-world outcomes. A positioning failure pattern should be a node connected to every company where Pulse observed it, strengthened when emails using that insight get replies, weakened when they don't. The graph needs domain expertise nodes, not just observation nodes.
data.user_insight: that document with all those words is actually better for claude to synthesize? and what's a knowledge graph for?
data.implication: expertise must be graph-native, not file-injected

data.goal: Map the knowledge graph flow in the email generation pipeline: understand how expertise and research data flows from research → graph → email generation
Design graph-native expertise system — move branding frameworks from static markdown into the knowledge graph with classification, retrieval, feedback loops, and emergent pattern detection

data.description: Designing graph-native expertise system for Pulse — moving branding frameworks from static markdown files into the knowledge graph with classification, retrieval, feedback loops, and emergent pattern detection
data.scope: Full architecture exploration of knowledge-graph.ts, brain.ts, generator.ts, critique.ts, prospect-memory.ts, industry-memory.ts, market-memory.ts, analyzer.ts, research/orchestrator.ts, schema.sql, and all 5 expertise markdown files

data.description: Graph-native expertise system fully built and wired. Expertise patterns are now knowledge graph nodes (not static markdown). 25 patterns seeded across 5 categories (positioning, website, visual identity, market, conversion). Classifier matches company traits against patterns during research. Retrieval pulls relevant patterns from graph for generator/critique prompts with confidence scores and company counts. Feedback loop reinforces patterns when emails get replies. Emergent pattern detection discovers new patterns from unclassified trait clusters. TypeScript clean.
data.modified_files: knowledge-graph.ts, prospect-memory.ts, generator.ts, critique.ts, brain.ts, index.ts, heartbeat.ts, schema.sql
data.key_insight: expertise lives in graph not markdown

data.description: Made pickRelevantExpertise() in brain.ts a legacy fallback rather than removing it. The function still works but is no longer called from generator.ts or critique.ts — those now use getGraphExpertise() directly. This means the markdown files still load into BrainContext but are only used if no expertise_pattern nodes exist in the graph (e.g., fresh install before first world model cycle).
data.why: Graceful degradation — if graph is empty, static expertise still works. Once seeded, graph takes over automatically.
data.alternative_rejected: Could have deleted pickRelevantExpertise entirely but that would break first-run before seeding

data.goal: Understand existing Gemini/Google AI infrastructure and embedding capabilities to plan adding embeddings

data.description: Completed comprehensive audit of current Gemini/Google AI infrastructure in Pulse
data.audit_scope: LLM setup, embeddings search, Google AI integration, packages, environment config, TypeScript setup, database schema
data.key_findings: Gemini integrated for Google Search grounding only, no embeddings yet, knowledge graph uses string-based similarity, ready for embedding infrastructure

data.description: Adding semantic embeddings to Pulse's knowledge graph using Gemini's gemini-embedding-001 model. Free tier, @google/genai already installed. Using 256-dimension vectors (not default 3072) to keep SQLite storage reasonable. Stored as JSON text in a new embedding column. Cosine similarity computed in JS. Replaces keyword-based findSimilarNodes() with semantic search. Also upgrades expertise classifier and emergent pattern detection.
data.why: Keyword matching misses synonyms. Semantic similarity catches 'overlapping messaging' = 'shared phrases'. Free API, already installed.
data.alternative_rejected: Default 3072 dimensions — too much storage for SQLite BLOB per row

data.description: Semantic embeddings fully wired into the world model cycle. The learning cycle now runs 8 steps: (1-5) synchronous keyword operations, (6) backfill embeddings via Gemini API, (7) semantic reclassification of undiagnosed traits, (8) semantic emergent pattern detection. Embedding failures are non-fatal — keyword classification still works as fallback.

Full audit of Pulse revealed 5 critical disconnections: (1) recordAngleTried never called when emails sent — world model doesn't know what angles were used, (2) processReplyInsight never called when replies come in — expertise patterns never get reinforced/weakened from outcomes, (3) replies.ts doesn't analyze sentiment — the sentiment column stays null, (4) follow-up emails don't receive world context or expertise patterns — only first emails do, (5) dead code `if (nodesEmbedded > 0 || true)` in world model cycle. The feedback loop exists in code but is completely unwired from the delivery pipeline.
data.impact: The entire learning loop is broken. Expertise patterns never get reinforced. The system sends email #100 with the same pattern confidence as email #1. This is exactly what Aslam criticized — processing companies without knowing them.

data.goal: Conduct thorough code audit of Pulse codebase to identify unused exports, missing error handling, SQL injection risks, type safety gaps, schema-code mismatches, circular dependencies, and dead code

data.description: Fixed 10 gaps across Pulse codebase in comprehensive audit: (1) Wired recordAngleTried into sendOneDraft — world model now knows what angles were used, (2) Wired processReplyInsight into recordReply — replies feed back into expertise pattern reinforcement, (3) Added deterministic sentiment analysis to recordReply, (4) Cleaned dead code in world model cycle, (5) Added world context + expertise to follow-up email generation, (6) Fixed industry retrieval query — was using wrong domain format (industry:X instead of joining through prospects table), (7) Fixed data integrity bug in promoteSuccessfulEmails — was storing critique_score instead of sent_email_id, (8) Removed dead pickRelevantExpertise from brain.ts, (9) Added writeFileSync safety with mkdirSync in intelligence.ts, (10) Verified end-to-end data flow and TypeScript clean build.
data.files_modified: queue.ts, replies.ts, index.ts, followup.ts, expertise-retrieval.ts, intelligence.ts, brain.ts

data.description: Feedback loops in autonomous systems need wiring verification at BOTH ends. Pulse had sophisticated functions for recording angles tried and processing reply insights, but neither was called from the delivery pipeline. The functions existed, had tests in concept, but the call sites were missing. Always trace data flow from trigger to storage to retrieval — if any link is missing, the intelligence doesn't compound.
data.why: Functions were built bottom-up (memory layer first, delivery layer later) and the integration step was missed

data.description: Complete multi-pass audit of Pulse codebase. All feedback loops verified connected. The system now has a closed loop: Research → Classify (expertise patterns) → Generate (world context + graph expertise) → Send (record angle) → Reply (sentiment → process insight → reinforce/weaken patterns) → Learn (world model cycle: industry profiles, market patterns, emergent detection, embeddings, semantic reclassify). Follow-ups also receive world context. TypeScript clean.

data.goal: Design implementation plan for Reply Intelligence and Temporal Market Memory features

data.description: Designing implementation plan for two major Pulse features: Reply Intelligence (LLM-powered reply analysis + briefings) and Temporal Market Memory (industry versioning, recency weighting, re-research, temporal patterns)
data.scope: Two features across ~15 files, touching delivery, memory, intelligence, CLI, and schema layers

data.goal: Implement Reply Intelligence + Temporal Market Memory — two features that close critical gaps in Pulse's feedback loop

data.description: Reply Intelligence + Temporal Market Memory — all 12 phases implemented and verified
data.what: Closed two critical gaps: (1) Reply Intelligence — LLM-powered reply analysis extracts structured signals (objections, interests, questions, timing, budget), builds actionable briefings with response strategies, wired into full feedback loop. (2) Temporal Market Memory — industry profile versioning with snapshots, recency-weighted pattern detection (exponential decay, halfLife=30), temporal patterns (monthly trends, day-of-week), re-research capability. All 15 files modified/created, TypeScript clean.
data.modified_files: replies.ts, conversations.ts, prospect-memory.ts, market-memory.ts, industry-memory.ts, pipeline.ts, dashboard.ts, schema.sql, setup.ts, index.ts, orchestrator.ts
data.new_commands: npm run pipeline briefing <id>, npm run pipeline reresearch <id>

data.goal: Fix all ESLint no-explicit-any and no-unused-vars errors in prospect-memory.ts

data.description: Fixed all 16 ESLint errors (1 no-unused-vars, 15 no-explicit-any) in prospect-memory.ts with proper types. Also fixed 17 TypeScript strict-null errors that were hidden behind `any` types.
data.approach: Created local interfaces (ProspectProfileRow, AuditStrength, AuditGap, AuditSection, DeepResearchSection, ProspectContent, CompetitorSet, BuildProfileAuditData) for all untyped data shapes. Used Prospect from db/index.ts for DB rows. Used Record<string, unknown> for recordInteraction data param. Fixed null-safety issues exposed by removing any types.

data.goal: Fix all ESLint errors in 6 smaller files: cli/approve.ts, cli/heartbeat.ts, db/seed.ts, delivery/learning.ts, research/competitors.ts, utils/llm.ts

data.description: Fixed all ESLint errors in 6 files: cli/approve.ts, cli/heartbeat.ts, db/seed.ts, delivery/learning.ts, research/competitors.ts, utils/llm.ts. Zero ESLint errors, zero new TypeScript regressions.
ESLint configured and entire codebase cleaned — 0 errors, 0 warnings
data.what: Set up ESLint with strict rules matching Development Principles pattern — no-explicit-any as error, no-unused-vars as error, no-floating-promises, no-misused-promises, require-await, no-unnecessary-type-assertion. Fixed 97 violations across 20+ files. Every `any` replaced with proper types. Every unused import/variable deleted. Added `npm run lint` and `npm run check` (tsc + eslint) scripts.
data.before: 97 problems (13 errors, 84 warnings) — no linting configured

data.description: ESLint rules: all warnings promoted to errors, matching Development Principles pattern
data.why: Punk Records Development Principles pattern says: 'No any types ever. No warnings, only errors. No eslint-disable comments.' Previous setup had no linter at all — 97 violations accumulated silently. Now enforced: no-explicit-any, no-floating-promises, no-misused-promises, require-await, no-unnecessary-type-assertion, eqeqeq, prefer-const, no-var.
data.alternatives: Could have used warn for any-types to ease migration — rejected because the pattern explicitly forbids warnings

data.goal: Build contact management system: Excel import, proper storage, auto-discovery during research
Understand how emails and contacts could be discovered during the research pipeline and identify opportunities for contact extraction
Design the implementation plan for Pulse's Contact Management System — Excel import, proper contact storage, and auto-growth during research

data.description: Designing Contact Management System plan for Pulse — Excel import, proper contact storage schema, and auto-growth during research pipeline
data.scope: Three requirements: 1) Parse XLSX/XLS files properly, 2) Better organization of prospect contacts with source tracking, 3) Auto-discover and save contacts found during research

data.description: Built Contact Management + Auto-Growth System for Pulse. Three capabilities: (1) Full Excel import via SheetJS — reads CSV, XLSX, and XLS files from Email leads/ folder. (2) Contacts table as address book with role classification and quality scoring. (3) Auto-discovery during research — extracts emails from scraped pages (including /contact), Gemini deep research, and competitor websites. Auto-promotes high-quality contacts to prospects during heartbeat cycle.
data.files_modified: src/db/schema.sql, src/db/setup.ts, src/db/index.ts, src/db/seed.ts, src/research/scraper.ts, src/research/orchestrator.ts, src/research/deep-research.ts, src/research/competitors.ts, src/cli/heartbeat.ts, src/cli/dashboard.ts, package.json

data.description: Added contacts table alongside prospects rather than restructuring prospects. Contacts are the address book, prospects are outreach targets. A contact gets promoted to prospect via quality threshold (0.6+). This preserves all 15+ files that depend on the prospects table.
data.why: prospects table is wired into orchestrator, delivery, conversations, memory, synthesis — restructuring would touch 15+ files for no gain. CRM lead-vs-opportunity pattern is cleaner.
data.alternatives_considered: restructure prospects table, add source columns only without separate contacts table

data.goal: Audit Contact Management system for gaps and inconsistencies

data.description: Completed comprehensive audit of Contact Management system - identified critical and medium issues

All passing tsc --noEmit + eslint clean.

data.key_findings: Backend-first CLI system, no existing frontend code

data.frontend_opportunity: Entire greenfield - can build modern UI layer

data.goal: Design complete architecture for Pulse real-time web dashboard — server structure, frontend structure, WebSocket protocol, build strategy

data.description: Built complete real-time web dashboard for Pulse. Backend: 8 server files (router, auth, WebSocket, static serving, events, webhooks, 9 API endpoints). Frontend: Vite + React 19 + Tailwind + Framer Motion + Zustand SPA with 7 views (Overview, Pipeline, Drafts, Activity, Delivery, Learnings, Revenue). Dark monochromatic design, spring physics animations, WebSocket live updates via dashboard_events polling. Heartbeat emits events at 8 lifecycle points. Dashboard server consolidates webhook server into single process. PM2 config updated. Everything compiles and builds clean.

Chose SQLite table polling (2s interval) over direct HTTP posting for heartbeat-to-dashboard communication. Zero coupling between processes, WAL handles concurrent access, events accumulate when dashboard is down.
data.alternatives: Direct HTTP post (tighter coupling), file watching (fragile), SQLite update_hook (same-process only)
data.why: Simplest, most decoupled. Heartbeat doesn't need to know dashboard exists.

data.description: Cleaned up dead webhook config: deleted src/delivery/webhooks.ts (superseded by src/server/webhooks.ts), removed WEBHOOK_ENABLED and WEBHOOK_PORT from config schema, removed webhooks script from package.json, cleaned up .env.example to have one clear dashboard server section

data.description: Dashboard redesign plan: kill 7 tabs, use overview-dominant layout with 3 views + 4 detail panels. Clash Grotesk self-hosted for display, Inter via @fontsource for body. [data-theme] CSS variable architecture for light/dark. ElimuAfrica-style max-w-6xl constrained layout with 2/3+1/3 grid. Staggered Framer Motion entrances. Bordered rounded-2xl cards.
data.why: Current dashboard uses generic tabs, no theme support, CDN fonts, edge-to-edge layout — none of which match Aslam's actual design patterns from portfolio and ElimuAfrica
data.alternatives_rejected: Floating sidebar (overkill for solo tool), pure monochromatic (hard to scan data), Poppins font (ElimuAfrica-specific, not personal brand)

All builds pass clean.

data.description: People panel stuck on "Loading..." — root cause was the server process was started before the contacts API route was added to src/server/api/index.ts. tsx doesn't hot-reload, so the running server had an old version without the /api/contacts route. Server restart fixed it. Also added error handling (.catch) to People.tsx so API failures show an error message instead of infinite loading.
data.secondary_issue: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY was set as empty string in shell env, overriding .env file since dotenv doesn't override existing env vars
data.fix: sourced .env before starting server; added .catch() to People.tsx useEffect

data.goal: Investigate why heartbeat pipeline stops after research and never synthesizes drafts

data.description: Pulse heartbeat pipeline stops after research and produces 0 drafts. Root cause is a schema migration gap: the live database is missing sequence_position on drafts and sent_emails tables, plus message_id on sent_emails. The code references these columns in multiple places. The synthesis orchestrator itself (saveDraft) does NOT reference sequence_position, so it can insert drafts — but the 2 errors logged per cycle are from intelligence.ts/promoteSuccessfulEmails (which queries sent_emails.sequence_position) and the learning phase. Additionally, the two researched prospects (domain.com and prospectone.io) are test/placeholder data: domain.com is a GoDaddy parked domain and prospectone.io has only 'Experts.' as its website content — both are effectively junk prospects that slipped past the disqualification gates.
data.schema_gap: drafts and sent_emails tables missing sequence_position column; sent_emails also missing message_id
data.code_expects: schema.sql line 36 drafts.sequence_position, line 53 sent_emails.sequence_position and message_id
data.db_has: drafts has no sequence_position; sent_emails has no sequence_position, no message_id (has old columns: opened, bounced, delivery_status)
data.error_source: intelligence.ts:34 queries s.sequence_position on sent_emails in promoteSuccessfulEmails(); followup.ts:145 inserts sequence_position into drafts; queue.ts:210 inserts message_id,sequence_position into sent_emails
data.prospect_quality: Audit #1 is domain.com (GoDaddy parked page), Audit #2 is prospectone.io with tagline 'Experts.' and no about/services text — both are junk but passed ICP gate because tagline is non-null
data.synthesis_itself: synthesis/orchestrator.ts saveDraft does NOT reference sequence_position - it could actually work, but errors in Phase 5 Learn (intelligence.ts promoteSuccessfulEmails) are being caught and logged as errors:2 in the cycle report

data.goal: Understand backup implementation: storage location, format, pruning mechanism, and restore capability

data.description: Analyzed backup implementation in Pulse project
data.findings: Backup system uses SQLite backup API via better-sqlite3, stores timestamped .db files, has pruning mechanism but NO restore mechanism
data.storage: data/backups/ directory with ISO timestamp naming
data.pruning: Keeps most recent 7 by default, deletes oldest on each backup
data.restore: No CLI command or API for restore
data.integration: Called automatically before each heartbeat cycle

data.description: Settings panel + cloud backup system implemented. Settings panel shows all config (identity, sending, heartbeat, integrations, database, backups). Gear icon in NavBar opens it. Backup system extended with R2 cloud support (upload after local backup, tiered retention: 24h/7d/30d), restore with safety backup, validation. All endpoints verified working.
Masked API keys in Settings integrations panel — shows first 8 + last 4 chars (e.g. sk-ant-a***vwAA) for Anthropic, Gemini, Resend, and Webhook Secret. IMAP shows host/user, R2 shows bucket name.

data.goal: Design implementation plan for making Pulse dashboard settings editable

data.description: Editable settings from dashboard — full implementation complete. .env writer (atomic), reloadConfig(), PATCH /api/settings with Zod validation, heartbeat constants moved to config, inline section editing UI with save/cancel.
data.modified_files: config.ts, router.ts, settings.ts (API), heartbeat.ts, types.ts, api.ts, Settings.tsx
data.key_decisions: Write directly to .env (single source of truth), atomic write via temp+rename, reloadConfig clears cache + re-reads .env, masked keys skipped on PATCH if value contains ***

data.description: Added R2 credentials to editable integrations section — Account ID, Access Key ID, Secret Access Key, and Bucket name. All masked on read, editable from dashboard. No need to touch .env for R2 setup.

data.goal: Continue from previous session - editable settings implementation is complete

data.description: Settings PATCH was failing when integrations section contained null values for unconfigured API keys. Fixed by: 1) Frontend strips null values before sending, 2) Backend Zod schema uses .nullish().transform() to accept and coerce nulls to undefined. Root cause: GET returns null for unconfigured masked keys, startEditing copies those nulls into draft, save sends them to PATCH which expected string|undefined not null.
data.root_cause: GET returns null for unconfigured keys, PATCH Zod schema only accepted string|undefined

data.goal: Review what has been built so far
Research all free tier hosting options for Pulse's Node.js app with always-on, SQLite, webhooks requirements

data.description: Completed comprehensive hosting research sprint for Pulse deployment - evaluated 25+ hosting options across free tiers, PaaS, VPS, and unconventional platforms
data.key_finding: Oracle Cloud Always Free ARM (4 cores, 24GB RAM, 200GB disk) is the best free option, followed by current GCP e2-micro. Cheapest paid fallback is Vultr VX1 at $2.50/mo or Hetzner CX23 at ~$3.49/mo. Most PaaS free tiers (Render, Koyeb, Railway) either sleep or lack persistent disk.

data.description: Fixed TypeScript compilation errors (MarketPattern casting in intelligence.ts), fixed ESLint errors (floating promises in settings.ts), initialized git repo, committed 151 files / 30,351 lines, pushed to GitHub as private repo GnSultan/pulse

data.goal: Design a phased implementation plan to transform Pulse from a pipeline to a brain — an intelligent, learning, empathetic outreach system

data.description: Completed comprehensive 5-phase implementation plan for transforming Pulse from pipeline to brain. Phase 1: Close Learning Loop (wire existing data). Phase 2: Prospect Empathy Model (deterministic inference from research data). Phase 3: Multi-Angle Strategy Engine (6 engagement approaches with weighted selection). Phase 4: Behavioral Intelligence (discomfort/impulse/action-scorer from Narrative Engine patterns). Phase 5: Dynamic Critique + Channel Awareness. Total: 6 new files, ~20 modified files, 0 new LLM calls in phases 1/2/4.
data.key_insight: Most value comes from Phase 1 (pure wiring) — all infrastructure exists, just disconnected

data.description: Designed 5-phase transformation plan to evolve Pulse from pipeline to brain: (1) Close learning loop - wire existing disconnected systems, (2) Prospect empathy model - make prospect the hero, (3) Multi-angle strategy - 6 engagement approaches instead of 1, (4) Behavioral intelligence - cross-pollinate Narrative Engine v2 deterministic consciousness, (5) Dynamic critique + channel awareness. Zero new LLM calls in phases 1, 2, 4. Only 5 new files. Mostly wiring existing infrastructure.
data.why: Pulse operates as a mechanical pipeline, not a brain. World model doesn't learn. Emails diagnose AT prospects instead of empathizing WITH them. Single engagement approach. Timer-driven instead of signal-driven. Philosophy demands: prospect as hero, authenticity, empathy.
data.alternatives: Could have done a full rewrite, but existing architecture is sound — just disconnected. Could have added more LLM calls for intelligence, but deterministic approach is cheaper and more debuggable.

data.description: Phase 1: Close the Learning Loop — complete. All 5 sub-phases implemented with zero new LLM calls.
data.changes: 1A: used_pattern_ids tracked on drafts, 1B: fast-track promotion + differentiated confidence boosts, 1C: learnings wired into send ordering + angle selection + exemplar weighting, 1D: incremental industry profile updates per-interaction, 1E: reinforceEdge with times_succeeded/times_failed tracking

Full audit of 5-phase Pulse transformation plan against codebase. Found and fixed 6 gaps: (1) angle learning query used wrong data source (best_angle vs angle_used), (2) send queue recorded wrong angle, (3) time-of-day optimization not wired into sends, (4) success_rate field missing on expertise nodes, (5) angle_exhausted discomfort type missing, (6) exemplar weighting was actually already implemented despite misleading function name.

data.verification: tsc clean, eslint clean, vite build clean, migrations applied

data.description: Deep execution trace audit of Pulse's brain. Found and fixed: (1) Silent error swallowing in empathy/industry feedback — bare catch blocks now log warnings. (2) executeProcessReplies was a no-op — now actually runs processReplyInsight on unanalyzed replies. (3) Reply analyzer used vestigial best_angle instead of actual angle_used from draft. (4) World context had no observability — now logs which intelligence layers are present/missing per prospect. All fixes verified clean (tsc + eslint + vite build).
data.key_insight: The system can complete processReplyInsight with zero reinforcement if diagnosed_patterns, used_pattern_ids, or industry is missing — it silently does nothing. Logging makes this observable now.

data.goal: Understand Pulse's follow-up email system: does it use brain intelligence or is it a dumb second email?

data.description: Follow-up email system is NOT dumb — it's surprisingly intelligent but with critical blind spots
data.what_works: Generates context-aware follow-ups using buildWorldContext (industry, market, prospect intelligence), checks angles_tried to avoid repeating angles, goes through full critique loop (gates + LLM scoring), uses getGraphExpertise for diagnosed patterns, uses exemplars with proper weighting, sequence_position correctly tracked at positions 2 and 3
data.critical_gaps: Follow-up prompt is STATIC — doesn't use selectAngle() for position 2-3. Reuses same opening structure every time regardless of prospect receptivity. Doesn't access the original email's angle to deliberately pick a different one. Door close (position 3) has NO special handling beyond word count. No knowledge of what specifically failed in the previous email. No retry loop like synthesizeWithCritique — generateFollowUp runs once, no gate failures trigger retrys. Prompt doesn't reference prospect's emotional_state or reply sentiment from interactions. No pattern weighting based on what worked previously.
data.data_flow: getFollowUpDue queries position 2/3 due dates → generateFollowUp loads audit + buildWorldContext + getGraphExpertise → critiqueEmail with dynamic weights → save draft → sendOneDraft → recordAngleTried + recordInteraction. Follow-ups are sent through same pipeline as email 1.

data.goal: Understand research data extraction pipeline and empathy model behavior under sparse data conditions
Verify exemplar promotion pipeline end-to-end: SQL joins, de-duplication keys, file format, brain cache invalidation, exemplar loading, critique calibration, and feedback parsing.

data.description: Traced complete research→empathy→synthesis pipeline. When data is thin, empathy model produces deterministic but hollow outputs with low readiness scores rather than useful narratives.
data.gap: Empathy model can produce empty shells with sparse data. All inference functions have fallback defaults and no minimum data threshold. When deepResearch returns empty/failed, customer_sentiment defaults to empty arrays. No reviews → readiness_score floors at 0.3. No social presence → channel_presence returns empty object. No competitors → competitive_pressure defaults to 'low'. Few pages scraped → brand_maturity set to 'nascent'. Email synthesis receives weak context that doesn't surface prospect's real situation.

data.description: Deep audit Pass 3 fixes complete: (1) Critique is now angle-aware — criteria descriptions adapt per angle type so question/story/compliment angles aren't scored against diagnosis expectations. (2) Follow-up system upgraded with angle selection via selectAngle(), exemplar injection via pickRandomExemplar(), and retry loop (2 attempts with critique feedback). Follow-up drafts now store angle_used, exemplar_used, and used_pattern_ids. (3) Empathy model now distinguishes thin data from real low-readiness via assessDataQuality() — thin-data prospects get readiness_score capped at 0.15 and generator sees "LIMITED DATA" header.
Follow-up retry loop uses MAX_FOLLOWUP_ATTEMPTS=2 (not 3 like email #1). Follow-ups are shorter/simpler, so less value in a 3rd retry. Reduces LLM cost while still catching gate failures.
data.why: Follow-ups are 80-100 words (position 2) or 30-50 words (position 3). Two attempts with critique feedback is enough — the third attempt for email #1 helps because those emails are more complex (150-250 words).

data.description: Pulse transformation complete and pushed to production. All 5 phases implemented: learning loop, empathy model, multi-angle strategy, behavioral intelligence, dynamic critique. Final bug fix (best_angle in intelligence API) committed. System is architecturally ready — needs real prospects and heartbeat cycles to activate.
